seoTitle: Create a Dashboard in Confluence: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to create a dashboard in Confluence in 2026 by combining multi-column sections, task macros, and live Jira feeds into one team homepage.
intro: When announcements and project metrics live scattered across a deep sidebar tree, your team loses operational visibility. This guide shows how to build a high-visibility dashboard homepage in Confluence in 2026.

steps.heading: Open your homepage canvas
steps.body: Open your space homepage, click the pencil icon, and open the canvas options drawer.

steps.heading: Go full width
steps.body: Move to the top center toolbar and click the full-width arrow toggle.

steps.heading: Add columns
steps.body: Click the section tool to add layout splitters and organize the page into a multi-column grid.

steps.heading: Add tracking macros
steps.body: In one column, type `/activity` or `/jira` to embed live stream updates and sprint trackers.

steps.heading: Add tasks and calendars
steps.body: In another column, type `/task` and `/calendar` to deploy checklists and shared release dates.

steps.heading: Add a roadmap view
steps.body: Insert a database block or Gantt timeline object to display high-level roadmap metrics.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review your visual hierarchy and click Publish to save the dashboard live.

faq.question: Will my dashboard update automatically as tasks change?
faq.answer: Yes, the rendering engine coordinates real-time activity feeds so dashboard components update as team members complete tasks.

faq.question: Can I pull live Jira data into my Confluence dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, typing `/jira` lets you inject live charts, activity streams, and filtered search results directly into a layout panel.

faq.question: How do I organize different types of content on a dashboard?
faq.answer: Using the section tool to build a multi-column grid lets you separate task lists, calendars, and Jira feeds into distinct panels.

faq.question: Will the dashboard look right on both desktop and mobile?
faq.answer: It's worth scaling your layout widths and checking the dashboard across desktop and mobile screens before publishing.
